titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jason Robards Papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes and Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 28, 2025 at 23:03

did.unittitle: Jason Robards papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection consists of papers documenting the life and work or American actor Jason Robards. Scripts, many of which are annotated, document many of Robard's roles on stage, film, and television. Playscripts contain several by Eugene O'Neill, including Hughie , Ah, Wilderness! , and A Moon for the Misbegotten . Correspondence consists of letters and other papers relating to a variety of theatrical, film, and television productions starring Robards; personal correspondence including correspondence with Robards's parents, Hope Maxine Milburn and Jason Robards Sr., letters received during Robards's naval service during the Second World War, condolence letters following his father's death, and fan correspondence; and business correspondence documenting potential roles and various projects, including Robards's charitable work. Other papers in the collecton include scrapbooks, press materials, clippings, a costume, props, realia, photographs, posters, lobby cards, artwork, annotated and inscribed books, awards, and audiovisual materials documenting Robards's acting career, as well as that of his father, Jason Robards, Sr. The collection also includes personal papers, which consist of calendars, planners, biographical materials, and memorials to Robards following his death in 2000.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 143-152 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 153 (health records): Restricted until 2071.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jason Robards Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the auhors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Shannon O'Connor Robards and Jake O'Neill Robards on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2020 and gift of Shannon O'Connor Robards and Jake O'Neill Robards, 2020.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of papers documenting the life and work or American actor Jason Robards. Scripts, many of which are annotated, document many of Robard's roles on stage, film, and television. Playscripts contain several by Eugene O'Neill, including Hughie , Ah, Wilderness! , and A Moon for the Misbegotten . Correspondence consists of letters and other papers relating to a variety of theatrical, film, and television productions starring Robards; personal correspondence including correspondence with Robards's parents, Hope Maxine Milburn and Jason Robards Sr., letters received during Robards's naval service during the Second World War, condolence letters following his father's death, and fan correspondence; and business correspondence documenting potential roles and various projects, including Robards's charitable work. Other papers in the collecton include scrapbooks, press materials, clippings, a costume, props, realia, photographs, posters, lobby cards, artwork, annotated and inscribed books, awards, and audiovisual materials documenting Robards's acting career, as well as that of his father, Jason Robards, Sr. The collection also includes personal papers, which consist of calendars, planners, biographical materials, and memorials to Robards following his death in 2000.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jason Robards Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into ten series: I. Scripts, 1951-1999. II. Correspondence, 1908-2002. III. Scrapbooks, press, and clippings, circa 1920s-2000. IV. Personal papers, 1914-2002. V. Costume, props, and realia, 1964-1966, undated. VI. Photographs, 1920-2000. VII. Posters, lobby cards, and artwork, 1856-1995. VIII. Annotated and inscribed books, 1866-1995. IX. Awards, 1955-2002. X. Audiovisual materials, 1965-2001.

bioghist.head: Jason Robards
bioghist.p: Jason Robards, Jr. (1922-2000) was a prolific American stage and film actor whose career stretched from the late 1940s, following his service in the United State Navy during the Second World War, until his death from lung cancer in 2000. On the stage, Robards repeatedly performed in plays by Eugene O'Neill, often in collaboration with director José Quintero, and originated the role of Jamie Tyrone, Jr. in Long Day's Journey into Night in 1956. He was nominated for eight Tony Awards, winning Best Performance by a Leading Actor in a Play for his work in The Disenchanted (1959). On screen, Robards appeared in over sixty films, including All the President's Men and Julia , for which he won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor in consecutive years, in 1976 and 1977, respectively. Robards was married four times, and had six children.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.unittitle: Scripts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Playscripts, 1951-1996; Filmscripts, 1962-1997; and Television scripts, 1963-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of scripts documenting Jason Robards's work as an actor in theater, film and television.

Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Production correspondence, 1967-1999; Personal and fan correspondence, 1908-2001; and Business correspondence, 1960-2002.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of professional correspondence related to Jason Robards's work as an actor; personal and fan correspondence, including family correspondence with Robards's parents Jason Robards Sr. and Hope Milburn; and business correspondence.

did.unittitle: Production correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Theater correspondence, 1966-1997; Film correspondence, 1971-1999; Television correspondence, 1967-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence related to the numerous theater, film, and television roles of Jason Robards.

Personal and fan correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a large amount of correspondence between Jason Robards, Jr. and his mother, Hope Maxine Milburn, letters received during his time in the U. S. Navy, well wishes following his 1972 automobile accident, and correspondence related to his many acting roles.

scopecontent.p: This series consists of props from film and television productions, a robe worn by Robards in a 1988 production of Long Day's Journey Into Night , and other realia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of photographs of or related to Jason Robards, including theater productions, family, school, and his Naval service.

Posters, lobby cards, and artwork

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Posters, 1872-1990, undated; Lobby cards, 1945-1968; and Artwork, 1856-1998, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of posters, lobby cards, and artwork related to Jason Robards, as well as his father, Jason Robards, Sr.

Annotated and inscribed books

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Books related to Eugene O'Neill, 1927-2001; and Other books, 1866-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of annotated and inscribed books. Many are either by or about Eugene O'Neill.

did.unittitle: Books related to Eugene O'Neill

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Plays and books by O'Neill, 1927-1988; Books about O'Neill, 1953-2001.

did.unittitle: Plays and books by O'Neill

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by date written or first published.

Strange Interlude (1928), first edition
Dynamo (1929), special edition, signed
Mourning Becomes Electra (1931), special edition, signed
Mourning Becomes Electra (1931), first edition
Ah, Wilderness! (1933), limited edition, signed
Ah, Wilderness! (1933)
More Stately Mansions (1939)
More Stately Mansions (1939), editor's inscribed note to Robards
The Iceman Cometh (1940), annotated copies
The Iceman Cometh (1940), limited edition
Long Day's Journey Into Night (1941), limited edition, signed and annotated by Robards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Script appeared to be used by Robards during theater run.

did.unittitle: Long Day's Journey Into Night (1941), signed and inscribed by Carlotta Monterey O'Neill
Long Day's Journey Into Night (1941)
A Moon for the Misbegotten (1941-1943), inscribed to Robards
A Touch of the Poet (1942), inscribed
The Calms of Capricorn (1981)
Books about O'Neill

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: Alexander, Doris. The Tempering of Eugene O'Neill
Bogard, Travis. From the Silence of Tao House , inscribed to Robards
Bowen, Croswell. The Curse of the Misbegotten , inscribed
Engel, Edwin. The Haunted Heroes of Eugene O'Neill
Floyd, Virginia. Eugene O'Neill at Work , inscribed by author to Robards
Josephson, Lennart. A Role: O'Neill's Cornelius Melody , author-inscribed note to Robards
Shafer, Yvonne. "Conversations with Actors & Directors Performing O'Neill"
Suffolk University. The Eugene O'Neill Review , Vol. 19, Nos. 1 & 2
Suffolk University. The Eugene O'Neill Review , Vol. 25, Nos. 1 & 2
Yale University Library. Inscriptions: Eugene O'Neill to Carlotta Monterey O'Neill (1960) , inscription to Robards
Other books

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: American Academy of Dramatic Arts annual catalogue
Bancroft, George. Memorial Address: Life and Character of Abraham Lincoln , first edition, signed and inscribed to Robards from Robert H. Clark
Barber, Rowland. The Night They Raided Minskey's
Behrman, S. N. But for Whom Charlie
Bradbury, Ray. Something Wicked This Way Comes , first edition, signed and inscribed by author to Robards
Bradbury, Ray. Green Shadows, White Whale , first edition, signed and inscribed by author
Dee, Peter. A Sea of White Horses , first edition, signed and transcribed by author
Fitzgerald, Percy. Henry Irving , first edition, signed by F. E. Celiase
Fowler, Gene. Good Night, Sweet Prince
Gardner, Herb. A Thousand Clowns , signed and inscribed by author
Grant, Ulysses. Personal Memoirs of U. S. Grant , first edition
Goodale, Katherine. Behind the Scenes with Edwin Booth , inscribed
Hellman, Lillian. Toys in the Attic , first edition, signed and inscribed by author
Hemingway, Ernest. For Whom the Bell Tolls , first edition
Hirschfeld, Al. The American Theatre , signed and inscribed
Hotchner, A. E. The White House: A Presidential Entertainment in Two Acts
Lewis, Leopold. The Bells , inscribed to Robards
Miller, Arthur. After the Fall , signed and inscribed by author
McCarthy, Mary. Sights and Spectacles
Schulberg, Budd. The Disenchanted , signed by cast and given to Robards from his father
Shakespeare, William. As You Like It , inscribed to Robards
Shakespeare, William. MacBeth , engraved with Robards' name, signed by cast
Vrbovska, Anca. The New Orlando Poetry Anthology , signed and inscribed by Julian Raeder to Robards
Awards

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of awards honoring Jason Robards.

did.unittitle: Village Voice's Off-Broadway award for Best Actor, certificate
Theatre World Award for Outstanding New Talent, statue
Antoinette Perry Tony Award for Best Actor in a Play for The Disenchanted , medallion
Antoinette Perry Tony Award nomination for Best Actor in a Play, certificate in album
Antoinette Perry Tony Award nomination for Best Actor in a Play, certificate in album
Drama Desk Award for performance in A Moon for the Misbegotten
Outer Critics Circle Award for outstanding performance in A Moon for the Misbegotten , certificate
The National Academy of Television Arts and Sciences Emmy Award nomination for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama or Comedy Special for A Moon for the Misbegotten , certificate
The Joseph Jefferson Awards nomination for Guest Artist Performance in Hughie , certificate
Golden Globe nomination for Best Actor in a Supporting Role in Julia from the Hollywood Foreign Press Association, certificate
The National Society of Film Critics Award for Best Supporting Actor for All the President's Men , certificate
Golden Globe nomination for Best Actor in a Supporting Role in All the President's Men from the Hollywood Foreign Press Association, certificate
State of Connecticut General Assembly recognition for Robards' Oscar win in All the President's Men , certificate
Emmy nomination for Outstanding Actor in a Limited Series for Washington: Behind Closed Doors from the Academy of Television and Arts & Sciences, certificate
Antoinette Perry Tony Award nomination for Outstanding Performance by an Actor in a Play for A Touch of the Poet , certificate in album
Golden Apple Award nominee for Star of the Year from the Hollywood Women's Press Club, certificate and correspondence
The British Academy Awards nomination for Best Supporting Actor in Julia , certificate

Emmy nomination for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Limited Series or Special for
F. D. R. The Last Year , certificate
Theater Hall of Fame award, medallion with ribbon
Kennedy Center Honors plaque for Robards
Doctor of Humane Letters from the University of Fairfield

A Tribute to Pearl Bailey and Jason Robards from the Catholic Actors Guild of America,
The Call Board booklet
O'Neill Birthday Medal from the Theater Committee for Eugene O'Neill, medal in red case
The National Arts Club Medal of Honor for Excellence in Drama/Theatre, medal in black case
National Council on Alcoholism Gold Key Award, matted certificate

The Washington Theatre Awards Society Helen Hayes Award for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Touring Production for
The Iceman Cometh , certificate
The Helen Hayes Award for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Touring Production or Prior to New York for The Iceman Cometh , medallion and stand
Los Angeles Drama Critics Circle For Distinguished Achievement in the Lead Performance for The Iceman Cometh , plaque
The Association of Yale Alumni's salute to Jason Robards, certificate
George Foster Peabody Award for Robards' narration on "Schizophrenia", certificate in album
Pell Award for Lifetime Achievement in the Arts from the state of Rhode Island, certificate in album
Distinguished Career Award from the Kennedy Center American College Theater Festival, medallion in glass
Common Wealth Award in Dramatic Arts
Dewhurst Lifetime Achievement Award from the American Council for the Arts
The Drama League Outstanding Contribution to the Theatre Award
The Players Club Edwin Booth Lifetime Award
Kennedy Center Honors, ribbon and plaque
Lucille Lortel Award for Outstanding Lifetime Achievement
R. Brinkley Smithers Award for Outstanding Contributions to the Field of Alcoholism
Roundabout Theatre Company Jason Robards Award for excellence in theatre
Glass bowl dedicated to Robards by the American College of Chest Physicians
Audiovisual materials

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Audiocassettes, 1978, undated; Film, 1978; Phonographic records, 1974, undated; Videocassettes, 1965-2001.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 143-152 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of audiovisual materials related to Jason Robards.

did.unittitle: An Inconvenient Woman , color timed, mixed unformatted
did.materialspec: No HiFi audio is present on the source. High amounts of hum are on the tape. Channels 1 and 2 are mono. Source has some visible tape damage and tracking issues due to low radio frequency.

did.unittitle: Beloved , Part 2
did.materialspec: HiFi audio is present. The audio has distortion and hum. HiFi audio is clean, but is split track. Channel 1 has dialogue and natural sound. Channel 2 has music.
